title: Bitcoin Stamps Rust Parser
description: Rules for understanding and working with the high-performance Rust parser component.

instructions: Used by the Transaction Filter to efficiently filter transactions.
Implements pattern matching for OP_MULTISIG and P2WSH outputs.
Manages memory-efficient caching with LRU implementation.
Utilities for parsing Bitcoin transaction structures.
Handles decoding of transaction inputs and outputs.
Extracts public keys and script data.
Implementation of ARC4 decryption for extracting stamp data.
Used to decrypt data from MULTISIG outputs.
Documentation covering usage, architecture, and performance considerations.
Reference for understanding the parser's role in the system.

patterns.description: Integration point with the Transaction Filter
instructions: Shows how the Rust parser is used by the Transaction Filter.
Helps understand the orchestration role of the Python code.
Contains the `filter_block_transactions` function that coordinates with the Rust parser.

conceptDefinitions.definition: High-performance component for filtering and initial parsing of transactions, used by the Transaction Filter.
Least Recently Used caching strategy used by the Rust parser to efficiently manage memory usage.
Original encoding format that stores stamp data in public keys within a multisignature script.
Pay-to-Witness-Script-Hash format used by OLGA for more efficient data storage.
Encryption algorithm used to decrypt data stored in MULTISIG outputs, using the previous transaction hash as the key.
Foreign Function Interface that allows the Python code to call the Rust implementation.
Process of identifying the 'stamp:' prefix in transaction data to validate stamp protocols.

relatedDocumentation.description: System architecture showing how the Rust parser fits into the overall system
Protocol specifications that the Rust parser helps validate
